questions.text: Which powerful Mongol ruler conquered a vast empire stretching from Asia to Europe?
questions.explanation: Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire, the largest contiguous land empire in history, spanning from Asia to Eastern Europe.

questions.text: In what decade was the internet created?
questions.explanation: The internet's precursor, ARPANET, was developed in the 1960s by the U.S. Department of Defense.

questions.text: What was the name of the first computer virus?
questions.explanation: Creeper was created in 1971 as an experiment, displaying 'I'm the creeper, catch me if you can!'

questions.text: Who was the world's highest-paid athlete in 2023?
questions.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo earned $260 million in 2023, making him the highest-paid athlete globally.

questions.text: Who wrote The Odyssey?
questions.explanation: Homer, the ancient Greek poet, is credited with writing both The Iliad and The Odyssey.

questions.text: What country is known as the Land of Fire and Ice?
questions.explanation: Iceland is called Land of Fire and Ice due to its volcanic activity and glaciers.

questions.text: What is the main language spoken in Brazil?
questions.explanation: Portuguese is the official language of Brazil, making it the largest Portuguese-speaking country in the world.

questions.text: Who painted Girl with a Pearl Earring?
questions.explanation: Johannes Vermeer painted 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' around 1665, often called the 'Mona Lisa of the North'.

questions.text: What year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, leading to German reunification.

questions.text: Who is the rival boy band in KPop Demon Hunters?
questions.explanation: The Saja Boys are the rival group in the KPop webtoon 'Demon Hunters'.

questions.text: What is the freezing point of water in Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water freezes at 0° Celsius (32° Fahrenheit) at standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.text: Who is the Greek god of the sea?
questions.explanation: Poseidon is the Greek god of the sea, earthquakes, and horses, brother of Zeus and Hades.

questions.text: What is the square root of 64?
questions.explanation: The square root of 64 is 8 because 8 × 8 = 64.

questions.text: What continent is the Sahara Desert on?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert covers most of North Africa and is the largest hot desert in the world.

questions.text: What is Selena Gomez's husband's name?
questions.explanation: Selena Gomez is in a relationship with music producer Benny Blanco (engaged as of 2024-2025).

questions.text: What's the longest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The femur (thigh bone) is the longest and strongest bone in the human body.

questions.text: What is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the capital of India, located within the larger Delhi metropolitan area.

questions.text: What are the three primary colors?
questions.explanation: The primary colors in traditional color theory are red, yellow, and blue.

questions.text: What is the largest island in the world?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the world's largest island, covering 2.16 million square kilometers.

questions.text: Who is known as the King of Pop?
questions.explanation: Michael Jackson is widely known as the King of Pop, with iconic albums like 'Thriller'.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Spain?
questions.explanation: Madrid is the capital and largest city of Spain, located in the center of the country.

questions.text: Who is known as the Queen of Soul?
questions.explanation: Aretha Franklin, known for 'Respect', is called the Queen of Soul.

questions.text: What animal has the longest lifespan?
questions.explanation: Glass sponges can live for over 10,000 years, the longest lifespan of any animal.

questions.text: What is the loudest animal on Earth?
questions.explanation: Sperm whale clicks reach 230 decibels, the loudest sound produced by any animal.

questions.text: What animal has the largest brain relative to body size?
questions.explanation: Dolphins have the highest brain-to-body ratio after humans, with complex intelligence and problem-solving abilities.

questions.text: What animal had the most powerful bite in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile crocodile has a bite force of up to 5,000 PSI, the strongest measured in any animal.

questions.text: How many hearts does an octopus have?
questions.explanation: Octopuses have three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, one pumps to the rest of the body.

questions.text: What is a group of pandas known as?
questions.explanation: A group of pandas is called an 'embarrassment' due to their awkward, clumsy movements.

questions.text: What animal has the longest tongue?
questions.explanation: Giraffes have 18-20 inch (45-50 cm) tongues, the longest of any land animal.

questions.text: Which ancient Chinese dynasty is often considered the 'golden age' of Chinese civilization?
questions.explanation: The Han Dynasty (206 BCE-220 CE) is considered China's golden age of cultural and technological advancement.
